sections.instruction: Complete the notes below. Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS AND/OR A NUMBER for each answer.

Questions 11-16: What comment does the manager make about each of the following steps in coffee processing? Choose SIX answers from the box and write the correct letter A-H next to questions 11-16. Questions 17 and 18: Choose TWO letters A-E. Questions 19 and 20: Choose TWO letters A-E.

options: B separates beans for different purposes

questions.content: Which TWO areas of the building are not part of the tour?
Which TWO things will each member of staff be provided with?

sections.instruction: Questions 21-26: Choose the correct letter A, B or C. Questions 27 and 28: Choose TWO letters A-E. Questions 29 and 30: Choose TWO letters A-E.

questions.content: What gives Tim the idea for his dissertation proposal?
options: A his grandmother asked him to record her language
B he heard about a linguistic project he wanted to join
C he learned about conserving languages on his course

questions.content: When talking about the impact of technology, Tim
options: A points out a problem caused by its spread
B explains how it can be used to conserve language
C emphasizes its importance for communication

questions.content: Tim and his tutor decide that in the introduction to his dissertation he should discuss
options: A the impact of media on language
B how globalization has helped remote communities
C the reasons why he talks to his grandmother in English

questions.content: What does Tim say about documenting dying languages?
options: A he believes writing a language down stops it from falling out of use
B he sees little point in trying to revive languages that are already dead
C he is unconvinced that his grandmother’s language can be saved

questions.content: Tim’s tutor explains to him that recording minority languages is
options: A something that schoolchildren can be involved in
B a way to allow future generations to experience them
C a useful tool for training language teachers

questions.content: When talking about language and culture Tim
options: A highlights why family traditions are important to him
B describes how the two subjects are linked together
C expresses a desire to learn about his family’s customs

questions.content: Which TWO areas of language does the tutor recommend Tim to focus on?
Which TWO approaches to recording do Tim and his tutor decide he should take?
options: A covering as much as possible
C keeping a record of times and places
E waiting a few days before listening back to it

sections.instruction: Questions 31-33: Choose the correct letter A, B or C. Questions 34-36: Complete the sentences below. Write ONE WORD only for each answer. Questions 37-40: Answer the questions below. Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS AND/ OR A NUMBER for each answer.

options: A where the sun rose and set
B the times of sunrise and sunset
C the length of the shortest day
A trees were planted on hill tops
C people began living in larger groups

questions.content:
34. The first clocks worked by making a small hole in a {{BLANK}} and allowing water to drip out.
35. Sundials measured time by noting where the sun’s shadow fell on the {{BLANK}} marked on the dial.
36. Workers in China and Japan changed activities when the {{BLANK}} of the incense changed.
